required_artifact_contract.durable_storage_required: R2 unless Max explicitly approves a named replacement durable target in this ledger before launch

entrypoint_policy.default: No existing modal_batch/compute.py entrypoint is approved for paid production work until G2 names an approved worker entrypoint here.
entrypoint_policy.preflight_guard_rule: Every paid Modal submitter must read this ledger and fail closed unless the current gate, approval flags, approved entrypoint, approved submit command, approved code-bundle hash, durable storage target, approved cells, approval nonce, launch mode, and allowed paid call count exactly match the requested launch. The submitter must use submitter_consume_and_reserve against the approved transactional store before creating calls, record per-cell reservation token hashes, and record launched call IDs after Modal returns. Every paid worker must run worker_verify_reserved_call at execution start before loading baseline data or running microsimulation, and must reject unless it has a matching single-use per-cell reservation token.

gates.name: Full-output-H5 worker implementation
evidence: src/reform_full_h5_worker.py implements run_reform_full_h5_cell and saves reform_full_h5/year=YYYY/reform=optionX/scenario.h5 before any aggregation.
src/reform_full_h5_artifacts.py validates full-H5 entity groups, row counts, exact columns, required weight variables, schema hash, object-store HEAD/GET, and SHA256.

src/reform_full_h5_contract.py implements submitter_consume_and_reserve, worker_verify_reserved_call, deterministic code-bundle hashing, single-use reservation tokens, and R2 conditional approval/reservation storage.

modal_batch/reform_full_h5.py is the guarded Modal submitter: it consumes the ledger before spawning calls, passes per-cell reservation tokens, requires object-store persistence, writes a submission manifest without raw tokens, and records launched call IDs.
Paid submitter uses only the canonical submit command derived from the actual arguments; a caller-supplied command override must match or the launch fails closed.
Paid submitter rejects non-dry launch_mode values other than sentinel or full, so the local contract test mode cannot spawn paid Modal calls.
compute_reform_full_h5_cell_remote recomputes the approved code-bundle SHA inside /app before calling run_reform_full_h5_cell and fails closed on mismatch.
compute_reform_full_h5_bundle_sha includes repository files, pinned package specs, policyengine[us] version, and the local policyengine-us tree hash when a local tree is mounted.
Full launches require approved_pip_freeze_sha256 from an accepted sentinel; workers compare the actual runtime pip freeze SHA before running microsimulation.
modal_batch/reform_full_h5.py imports cleanly with Modal installed: uv run --with modal python -c "import modal_batch.reform_full_h5; print('import-ok')".
R2ConditionalApprovalStore.consume_reservation reads the reservation object, validates nonce/cell/launch-mode/worker/code-bundle payload fields, then conditionally writes the consumed marker.
run_reform_full_h5_cell validates the actual R2 bucket/key prefix is under approved_durable_storage_target before upload.
run_reform_full_h5_cell validates required object-store config and approved R2 target before building reforms or running Microsimulation.
run_reform_full_h5_cell compares the expected schema manifest SHA and baseline dataset manifest SHA to the ledger-approved SHAs before accepting an artifact.

run_reform_full_h5_cell preloads and semantically validates the approved expected schema manifest, including entity schema structure and required weight-column presence, before running Microsimulation.
Guarded workers require an expected_schema_manifest_path and always validate the saved H5 against it.

Baseline H5 inputs are preapproved through approved_baseline_dataset_manifest and approved_baseline_dataset_manifest_sha; the worker verifies each year dataset H5 SHA before constructing the microsimulation.
Baseline dataset manifest records must include metadata_path and metadata_sha256; worker verifies that metadata and uses it for tax-assumption reform construction.
approved_worker_sha is verified against the full-H5 worker file hash in both submitter and worker guard calls.

Saved metadata.json contains full runtime provenance, selected package versions, pip freeze SHA/list, baseline metadata path/hash when present, data identifiers, policyengine identifiers, and Trustees source metadata.

Saved metadata.json contains an object-store validation block before upload; upload validation GETs and SHA-checks both scenario.h5 and metadata.json, with final GET/HEAD details returned separately to avoid self-referential metadata hashes.
Object-store upload uses conditional create writes and a complete.json marker; repeated keys fail instead of overwriting.
save_complete_microsimulation_h5 fails on any unapproved skipped variable instead of marking a partial full-H5 successful.

The full-H5 worker metadata records output_h5_sha256, output_h5_size_bytes, modal_volume_path, R2 keys/validation, dataset hash, tax-assumption contract, and flags proving no baseline aggregate metrics were computed before H5 save.
code_bundle_paths covers every copied Python file under src/**/*.py and modal_batch/**/*.py, plus pyproject.toml and uv.lock, so changes to copied executable code invalidate the approved bundle.

record_spawned_call writes each spawned Modal call ID to the canonical ledger immediately after spawn and before optional dashboard URL enrichment; record_launched_call_ids finalizes only after the full approved count matches the spawned records.
exit_criteria: Worker saves PolicyEngine full output dataset H5 for one reform cell.
Worker does not call aggregate/baseline scoring helpers.
Worker records provenance, hashes, R2 key, and Modal backup path.
Every paid submitter calls a shared preflight-and-consume guard before creating Modal calls.
Every paid worker calls the same guard at execution start before loading baseline data or running microsimulation.
Approved code-bundle hash covers worker, submitter, guard, reform definitions, dependency lockfile/package tree, and packaging metadata.
A named transactional approval/reservation store is implemented for paid launches.
An expected full-output schema manifest is pre-approved before any candidate reform H5 is validated.

uv run --with modal python -c "import modal_batch.reform_full_h5 as m; paths=m.code_bundle_paths(m.LOCAL_PROJECT_ROOT); assert 'src/reform_full_h5_worker.py' in paths; assert 'src/reform_full_h5_contract.py' in paths; assert 'modal_batch/compute.py' in paths; assert 'modal_batch/reform_full_h5.py' in paths; print('bundle-paths-ok', len(paths))" -> bundle-paths-ok 25

Static grep over src/reform_full_h5_worker.py, src/reform_full_h5_contract.py, src/reform_full_h5_artifacts.py, and modal_batch/reform_full_h5.py found no legacy aggregate helpers, no year_runner import, no np.dot, and no direct weight multiplication patterns.

tests/test_reform_full_h5_contract.py covers single-use approval consumption, direct-worker rejection without reservation, code-bundle SHA drift rejection, worker SHA mismatch rejection, concurrent submitter consumption allowing exactly one success, launched-call count validation, R2 URI parsing, R2 reservation-existence enforcement, and full-launch pip-freeze approval enforcement.

tests/test_reform_full_h5_artifacts.py covers expected schema validation, malformed expected-schema manifest rejection before compute, required household weight rejection, object-store upload/HEAD/GET SHA validation for scenario.h5, metadata.json, and complete.json, metadata SHA mismatch rejection, no-overwrite behavior, and required-weight inspection.

tests/test_reform_full_h5_worker.py covers unapproved skipped-variable failure, approved R2 target prefix enforcement, baseline dataset manifest SHA validation, and baseline H5 SHA mismatch rejection.

tests/test_reform_full_h5_submitter.py statically requires the submitter guard, call-ID recording, remote bundle rehash, runtime fingerprinting, policyengine-us tree hashing, full-H5 remote worker spawn, object-store requirement, schema and baseline manifest payload forwarding, and absence of legacy aggregate paths.

Focused tests pass.
grep/static audit proves forbidden helpers are absent from the full-H5 worker path.
Static audit proves every paid submitter and worker is protected by the shared ledger preflight-and-consume guard.

Static audit proves the guard rejects reused approval nonces, consumed approvals, mismatched code-bundle hashes, mismatched cells, mismatched launch mode, missing durable storage targets, and call counts above the approved count.
Concurrent test proves two simultaneous submitter_consume_and_reserve calls against the same approval_nonce cannot both succeed.
Worker guard tests prove direct invocation with only approval_nonce fails and a matching per-cell reservation token is required.
Static validation proves schema checks use the approved expected schema manifest, not a schema hash derived from the candidate artifact.
